highlights.text: I wrote back last Monday at 4pm (before the sell) that: “ Bear case tomorrow: Short-term, begins below 6453” and “If 6453 fails, we start down.” Tuesday morning, ES lost 6453 and began a sell that took ES down to 6362 by Wednesday morning.
I wrote on Thursday at 4pm that: “A Failed Breakdown of today’s 6370 daily low is always of interest.” I also wrote on Thursday at 4pm: “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
I call this sequence “elevator down” sell, into short squeeze.
My specialty is trading the squeezes and in ES, the squeezes always occur on my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
Failed Breakdowns are how institutions accumulate, and after a violent sell ES undercuts a low, traps shorts, and squeezes.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into three monster Failed Breakdowns we had last week from Wednesday into Friday.

post_title: SPX Is Coiled Tight After Friday's Squeeze. New All Time Highs Ahead, or Sell? August 26 Plan
